meaning: adj.不信任的,怀疑的,可疑的
examples: The tailor was distrustful, went down, and asked, "Goat, have you had enough?"
Even those passengers who were most distrustful of themselves pluck up amazingly.
But she says the destruction of her school leaves her distrustful there will be a lasting breakthrough.
"They yearn for authenticity and are distrustful of gimmicks, " says Phil Lempert, food trend expert and founder of Supermarketguru.com.
